summary: The text discusses cipher interpretation rules, including reversing letter order and using similar letters to derive keys from ciphers, with references to specific letter sequences and methods for decoding.

entities.name: reversed alphabet order
entities.description: Using reverse sequence (e.g., DCBA) as a cipher method
entities.quote: retrogando ordine, recto ABCD, retrogra-dout DCBA

entities.name: similar letters rule
entities.description: Identifying and using repeated or similar ciphered letters to deduce keys
entities.quote: regula for similar letters, in which I proceed straight ahead with the key

entities.name: CCC
entities.description: Four repeated letters (CCCC) identified as helpful for decoding
entities.quote: initio four similar letters CCCC, which is the greatest help for us

entities.name: lead lamellae
entities.description: Thin sheets of lead used to inscribe secret messages
entities.quote: letters are placed in leaden lamellae, closed in the middle with wax

entities.name: wax seal
entities.description: Used to close and secure lead lamellae containing secret messages
entities.quote: closed in the middle with wax, and sealed

entities.name: papyrus roll
entities.description: A rolled papyrus container used to conceal and transport secret writings
entities.quote: a papyrus-lined roll, wrapped with leaden pile

entities.name: Cralem Servier Defpotum
entities.description: Recipient of secret correspondence mentioned in historical context
entities.quote: letters were sent to Cralem Servier Defpotum

entities.name: columbae memory
entities.description: Reference to pigeons' memory as a metaphor or method in cryptographic practice
entities.quote: Themistius says among animals columbz memory

entities.name: interlaced letters (equantes)
entities.description: Letters interlaced according to specific rules (e.g., VACE) for decoding
entities.quote: four letters are interlaced (equantes), one (cilicer intercalated) VACE

entities.name: STV rule
entities.description: A decoding rule involving the sequence STV to determine placement or key
entities.quote: by the third rule STV to say place
